title: Belgium Passport Application (Adults and Children 6 Years and Older)
description: The Federal Public Service Foreign Affairs, Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation's (SPF Affaires étrangères / FOD Buitenlandse Zaken, branded publicly as "diplomatie.belgium.be") "Demande de passeport" (Formulaire pour adultes et enfants à partir de 6 ans) — the ordinary passport application for adults and children aged 6 and older, filed at a Belgian consular post abroad or a Belgian municipal administration. Fetched directly and unauthenticated as a static PDF from diplomatie.belgium.be. Opens Belgium as a new jurisdiction in the registry (GOV-4645, "GovSchema Standard Research"), picked up from a candidate scouted and banked during GOV-4638's parallel new-jurisdiction pass alongside Hungary and Tunisia. This edition is the French-language specimen; the same form is also published in parallel, functionally identical Dutch- and German-language editions. This schema does not submit the application itself; the live source is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Belgium or its Federal Public Service Foreign Affairs, Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation.

authority.name: Federal Public Service Foreign Affairs, Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation (Service public fédéral Affaires étrangères, Commerce extérieur et Coopération au Développement)

source.documentRef: Demande de passeport (Formulaire pour adultes et enfants à partir de 6 ans), July 2024 edition

verification.notes: See VERIFICATION.md for the full write-up: source fetch/hash, the relative-URL host-resolution pitfall this cycle hit before reaching the live PDF, node-canvas visual checkbox confirmation, document structure, disclosed exclusions, and every scoping/judgment call.

steps.title: My Personal Data or the Child's
Date, Signature, and Legal Representative(s) for a Minor

fields.name: lastName
fields.label: Last Name
fields.description: Printed with its own sub-note: "(de jeune fille pour femmes mariées)" — maiden name, for married women.

fields.name: spouseName
fields.label: Spouse's Name
fields.description: Not gated behind maritalStatus — unlike this registry's usual adjacency-based conditional inference (e.g. tn/dgsn/passport-application's spouse block), this field sits several lines above the État civil (marital status) group with no adjacent or cross-referencing label linking the two, so no requiredWhen link is asserted; see VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: placeOfBirth
fields.label: Place of Birth (and Country)
fields.description: Printed as a single blank line covering both the place and country of birth, unlike some other registry sources that split these into separate fields — modelled as one field matching this specimen's own layout.

fields.name: otherNationalities
fields.label: Other Nationality/Nationalities
fields.description: This form's own item 8 pre-prints "belge" (Belgian) as a fixed value beside "Nationalité(s):", not an applicant-editable field — this schema does not model "belge" itself as an input. The adjoining blank "Autre(s):" line, for any additional (dual) nationality, is modelled here as an optional supplementary disclosure, consistent with this registry's treatment of comparable fields elsewhere (e.g. tn/dgsn/passport-application's otherNationalities).

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital Status
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item 9 — "État civil": ☐ Célibataire ☐ Marié(e) ☐ Divorcé(e) ☐ Veuf/Veuve

fields.name: address
fields.label: Address (Street, Number, Postal Code, Municipality, Country)
fields.description: Printed as a single blank line combining street, number, postal code, municipality, and country, unlike this registry's other address blocks that split a printed multi-column table into separate fields (e.g. tn/dgsn/passport-application's four-column address) — modelled as one field matching this specimen's own single-line layout.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item 10 — "Adresse (rue, numéro, code postal, commune, pays)"

fields.name: phoneNumber
fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: This source carries no asterisk/mandatory-marking convention anywhere; phoneNumber and email are modelled required as the form's own sole named contact channels for the consular post to reach the applicant about appointment scheduling, flying-consul missions, and passport pickup — not treated as optional supplementary disclosures. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email Address
fields.description: Same requiredness reasoning as phoneNumber above.

fields.name: passportLanguage
fields.label: First Language of the Passport
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, item 4.1 — "Première langue du passeport": ☐ Français ☐ Nederlands ☐ Deutsch

fields.name: passportType
fields.label: Type of Passport
fields.description: The 66-page option is printed with its own bolded caveat, "Attention: surcoût à payer!" (a surcharge applies) — recorded here for context, not as a separate fee field, since the form prints no fee amount.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, item 4.2 — "Type de passeport": ☐ ordinaire 34 pages ☐ ordinaire 66 pages

fields.name: biometricDataRegistrationLocation
fields.label: Biometric Data Registration Location
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, item 4.3 — "Enregistrement des données biométriques": ☐ dans mon poste consulaire ☐ lors de la mission flying consul prévue à …

fields.name: flyingConsulMissionLocation
fields.label: Flying Consul Mission Location/Date
fields.description: The dotted blank line printed directly after "lors de la mission flying consul prévue à …" on the same option line, unlike spouseName above — here the blank is directly adjacent to and part of the same printed option, so this schema does assert the conditional link.

fields.name: passportCollectionMethod
fields.label: Passport Collection Method
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, item 4.4 — "Mode de retrait du passeport": ☐ Je souhaite recevoir le passeport par courrier ☐ Je viendrai chercher le passeport en personne ☐ Je donnerai procuration à une tierce personne ☐ Je n'ai pas encore décidé

fields.name: longTermDataRetentionOptIn
fields.label: Optional Long-Term Data Retention (7 Years)
fields.description: A single opt-in checkbox: the applicant may expressly request their (or the child's) personal data be retained for 7 years, rather than the standard 3 months, for a future replacement passport in case of loss/theft/manufacturing defect. The form's own note states this option is only available at consular posts, not municipal administrations.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, item 5 — "Conservation facultative de longue durée des données"

fields.name: applicantSignature
fields.label: Applicant's Signature
fields.description: The form's own footnote clarifies "demandeur" (applicant) is used here in gender-neutral (epicene) form.

fields.name: legalRepresentative1Name
fields.label: Legal Representative 1: Name(s)
fields.description: This form prints no separate "is this application for a minor" discriminator field, so this bounded 2-slot legal-representative block (matching the specimen's own two printed blank rows) is modelled as optional throughout rather than conditionally required via a fabricated discriminator; see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Declaration block, row 1 — "Pour le mineur, signature du/des parent(s), tuteur(s) ou du représentant légal" table, column "Nom(s)"

fields.name: legalRepresentative1RelationshipToMinor
fields.label: Legal Representative 1: Relationship to the Minor
fields.sourceRef: Declaration block, row 1, column "Lien avec le mineur"

fields.name: legalRepresentative2Name
fields.label: Legal Representative 2: Name(s)
fields.sourceRef: Declaration block, row 2 — same table, second blank row

fields.name: legalRepresentative2RelationshipToMinor
fields.label: Legal Representative 2: Relationship to the Minor
fields.sourceRef: Declaration block, row 2, column "Lien avec le mineur"
